回上一頁	國立金門大學	 
 	教學綱要	 

部別 : 日間部學士班	112學年度第1學期	列印日期 : 2026/05/24
科目名稱 : 國際禮儀 (International Etiquette)	開課班級 : 應英一	學　　分 : 2.0	授課時數 : 2.0
授課教師 : 周晏安	必選修 : 選修	 	 


1.教學目標
By the end of the course students will have: -handled and joined a meeting and the appropriate way in communication. -acquainted with international etiquette so that they have the knowledge in dealing with social functions. -reflect on their own cultural identities and consider alternate views. -learned about different cultureand learned about internationalists -understood the connection between culture and communication -given at least one presentation incorporating learned cultural concepts or international business ettiques 


2.教學綱要
English is widely used as a common language in international society. For students who major in International Affairs, it is a basic tool to deal with the matters relevant with it. Followed by the trend of globalization, transnational meetings and negotiations in every business field is inevitable and getting more and more frequent. Better communication in English is a necessity in business developing.
This course will give students an idea of appropriate way to deal in a meeting in which foreigners are involved. The points will be focused on how to host a meeting, how to make a speech, the manner in discussion and asking questions. On the other hand, etiquette is also an important criterion in participating a meeting.
Etiquette has to be studied in cultural context. Every culture has etiquette of its own. The reason why the Western etiquette is becoming the most popular one which used in social functions, has something to do with the spread of West Europe and US power started in fifteenth century, politically and economically, until today.
The course will introduce principal ideas in how to act in a social function.

6.成績評定及課堂要求
Homework/ Reflections: 25% Midterm: 25% Final group presentation: 25% Attendance, class participation and presentation: 25% 
1.Self-respect at all aspects. 2. Late work will not be graded unless you’ve made prior arrangements with me.
